creator: walt@relnor.com
text:
If you won't insist, then I will :)
I think this is important for  consistency. Generally we find in the 
ports tree that most ports follow the lower-case-only rule.  Certainly 
all don't, but the majority do, and FreeBSD's reliability in consistency 
is one of the things that I love about it.  A good example is 
databases/phpmyadmin -- it's not databases/phpMyAdmin, and thusly will 
show up when someone sends out "locate phpmyadmin".

Mark Linimon wrote:
> On Wed, Sep 03, 2008 at 10:25:50AM +0300, Walter Venable wrote:
>   
>> The renaming is only a bonus, since the port needs to be renamed anyway,
>> I figured we could decapitalize it for consistency.
>>     
>
> This is what I'm disagreeing with; I don't see the reason to decapitalize,
> since it's been that way for a long time.  However, I'm not going to
> insist.
>
> mcl
>
